notes:
Game launches, textbox when entering profile name causes crash. Circumvented by creating a profile on Windows and transferring it to the steam proton compatdata folder.
A few texts do not have their fonts loaded in the menu.
Menus stutter significantly.
Upon starting a game, roughly 3/4 times the game would crash during loading/intro cutscene, and 1/4 times load into the gameplay proper, only to crash after less than 30 seconds. Crashed my entire system more than once during testing. Borked, sadly, for me.

Disabling Esync has only, if at all, marginally improves chances of getting into proper gameplay, but does not prevent crashing after a short while.
Disabling D3d11 prevented the game from opening at all.
Forcing Wine D3d11 causes the game window to be entirely black, and close after an exceedingly short time, you don't even get to the start screen.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes: Bronze because proton performance poor, flickering, crashing, BUT I GOT IT RUNNING PERFECTLY, with high framerates. Read on if interested: 0. Install normally through steam play (linux steam). Install lutris through the repo and add the game manually. SELECT WINDOWS STEAM as the runner! Lutris does the rest (installs a win steam for it). All of this is done to gain access to finer control of the versions involved. After instal: 1.boot to windows, make a profile (if you want, you can run the same exact install from windows, just add the library if it's on a external usb, no copying needed) 2. right click on icon and select from runner options: wineversion 4.0-rc4 staging (system, install this version if you don't have it, you may have to enable the winehq repo for it). 3. For performance select DXVK ON - VERSION 0.30 (newest versions flicker and crash), emulate virtual desktop ON, with your native resolution. 4. Then once in the game select windowed mode. You can move your window with alt + f7. You might have a small upper taskbar depending on your distro, but that's all. That's it. Unfortunately fullscreen does still flicker, but small upper taskbar doesn't bother me. Alt-tabbing and meta+s work perfectly and it's totally stable. A newer DXVK might work, but at least 0.81 and newer flicker and crash with poor performance on top.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes: Ran perfectly, multiplayer works, DS4 works. Font was a little hard to read, illegible in a few menus.

title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes:
Game works quite well save for a text input problem that rises in two occasions: 1) Chat text (easily avoidable), and 2) profile creation (this can't be avoided!). As of now, the only way to create a profile is to create it on Windows and copy it to Linux.

After profile creation, game works almost perfect. There are some text characters missing though (for difficulty selection).
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes: The game can't find save data folder and when selecting game slot the game just hangs.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes: A profile does indeed need to be created on Windows. DXVK 0.96 has good performances but will crash the game occasionally in solo and frequently in co-op multiplayer. WINED3D11 is generally acceptable though the game will feel sluggish when a lot is happening on the screen (enemies, massive weapons in action, etc). In-game settings are best without anti-aliasing and without shadow. Anti-aliasing increases the frequency of crashes. Any text input including chat will crash the game. It's possible to communicate through predefined texts (using dpad on a controller).
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

gpu: ATI Technologies Inc. AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT
notes: first you need a savegame from windows. then just use "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" (without the "") because wined3d works way faster than dxvk in this case and wined3d doesn't crash all the time

title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes: Since Proton 4.11-2 it works out of the box with DXVK (D3D11 to Vulkan translation). I didn't had to use any saved game, I could create in game a new one. Tested with the new Mesa ACO compiler on Plasma Wayland.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes: I can confirm that since proton 4.11-2 the game is fully playable. However if I run it with vulkan DXVK it would hang just as the previous version. However if you launch the game in OpenGL mode with PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command% the game will be fully playable with no lag even in the most demanding stages at full graph settings. Thanks Valve

title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair

notes:
DXVK on this game doesn't work , use PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command% to play. after that , the only problem is some text being replaced by squares (ex : difficulty settings) but the description of said items still work , so it's not too much of a problem.
it's also to note that this game can run at higher refresh rates on linux without any problem (75hz here) while on windows it's eternally stuck to 60hz.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 4.1 The Shadow of New Despair
